questions.question: What does the dot product of two vectors tell you geometrically?
options: The area of the parallelogram they span
The projection of one onto the other; related to the cosine of the angle between them
The cross product magnitude
The sum of their components
questions.explanation: The dot product u·v = |u||v|cos θ. When normalized, it gives the cosine of the angle. A dot product of 0 means the vectors are perpendicular. This is fundamental to lighting calculations (Lambert's law uses dot product of light direction and surface normal).

questions.question: Why is matrix multiplication not commutative (AB ≠ BA in general)?
options: It's a bug in the definition
Matrices represent transformations; applying rotation then translation gives different results than translation then rotation
Only square matrices commute
It actually is commutative for all real matrices
questions.explanation: Matrix multiplication represents function composition. Rotating a point 90° then translating right gives a different position than translating right then rotating 90°. This is why the order of transformations in a graphics pipeline matters: Model → View → Projection.

questions.question: What does det(A) = 0 mean for a matrix A?
options: A is the identity matrix
A collapses at least one dimension; no unique solution exists for Ax = b; A has no inverse
A is diagonal
All entries of A are zero
questions.explanation: det(A) = 0 means the transformation represented by A maps some non-zero vectors to zero — it collapses dimension. The matrix is 'singular': Ax = b has either no solution or infinitely many. In graphics, this means your projection matrix is degenerate. In ML, this means your feature matrix has linearly dependent columns.

questions.question: How does Google's PageRank use eigenvectors?
options: It computes eigenvalues of the HTML
The PageRank vector is the dominant eigenvector of the web graph's transition matrix — the steady-state probability of landing on each page
It uses eigenvectors to compress images
Eigenvectors are not used in PageRank
questions.explanation: The web graph is a Markov chain. The transition matrix M has a dominant eigenvalue λ=1, and its corresponding eigenvector gives the stationary distribution — the probability of being on each page after infinite random walks. Power iteration (repeatedly multiplying M by a vector) converges to this eigenvector.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of solving Ax = b via Gaussian elimination for an n×n system?
options: O(n)
O(n²)
O(n³)
O(2ⁿ)
questions.explanation: Gaussian elimination has three nested loops, each up to n, giving O(n³). This is why large systems use iterative methods (conjugate gradient, GMRES) which can be faster for sparse matrices. LU decomposition is also O(n³) but allows solving multiple right-hand sides in O(n²) each.
